main.py.summary: Entry point for Telegram bot application. Creates bot instance, registers command and message handlers, and starts FastAPI server (if configured). Contains application lifecycle management.

handlers/start.py.summary: Handles /start command. Retrieves or creates user in database, sends welcome message, and displays available commands. Critical for user onboarding.

handlers/help.py.summary: Handles /help command. Provides formatted list of available commands with descriptions. Helps users discover bot capabilities.

handlers/message.py.summary: Main message handler for all non-command text messages. Delegates user input to AI logic service. Core routing layer between Telegram and business logic.

services/ai_logic.py.summary: Core intelligence module that processes user input and generates responses. Contains all bot features, command parsing, and external API orchestration. This is the brain of the bot.

services/api_client.py.summary: External API client module. Encapsulates all HTTP requests to third-party services (CoinGecko, weather, etc.). Centralizes API calls for easy modification.

services/database.py.summary: Database service layer. Provides high-level database operations for user management, alerts, and other persistent data. Abstracts SQLAlchemy complexity.

core/database.py.summary: Database connection and session factory. Manages SQLAlchemy engine, connection pooling, and provides session objects. Contains init_db() for table creation.

models/user.py.summary: SQLAlchemy model for users table. Supports both Telegram users (auto-created) and email-based users (manual registration). Unifies user management.

config.py.summary: Application configuration module. Loads and validates environment variables using Pydantic settings. Provides type-safe config access.

utils/logger.py.summary: Logging configuration for the bot. Sets up structured logging with timestamps, levels, and formatting. Provides logger instance used throughout codebase.

buildpublish.py.summary: Build and publish script for deploying bot changes. Installs dependencies, clears cache, restarts PM2, and re-registers webhook. Critical for development workflow.
